ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Vietnamese chicken hot pot with lá giang. The closest defensible FoodOn class is 'hot pot food product' (FOODON_03315595), used as an approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as the primary protein anchor for this composite hot pot dish. No FoodEx2 code exists for the complete dish; this code anchors the dominant protein component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Vietnamese chicken hot pot with sour soup creeper. A0FJP (cooked chicken meat) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for this specific Vietnamese hot pot dish. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for regional composite dishes of this type.

main_ingredients: lá giang leaves (sour soup creeper, Aganonerion polymorphum)
allergens: gluten (vermicelli — rice-based, typically gluten-free; verify brand)

serving.description: 1 individual bowl serving from a shared hot pot
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical individual serving drawn from a shared hot pot is approximately 400 g, comprising broth, chicken pieces, vegetables, and a moderate portion of vermicelli noodles.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken soup with vegetables, homemade-style (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); supplemented with FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for lá giang leaf contribution and HPB Singapore Foodo for regional composite dish reference
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; FAO/INFOODS; Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No approved source contains a direct entry for 'Chicken Hot Pot with Sour Soup Creeper (Lẩu Gà Lá Giang)'. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central chicken soup with vegetables (FDC ID 171534) as the primary structural analogue, adjusted for the sour broth character contributed by lá giang leaves (Aganonerion polymorphum), tomatoes, and lemongrass. The input record values (energy 120 kcal, protein 8 g, fat 5.33 g, carbohydrate 10 g, sodium 300 mg per 100 g) are broadly consistent with USDA chicken soup analogues and are retained with medium confidence. Micronutrient values (calcium, iron, vitamin C, cholesterol) are cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy chicken soup entries. Potassium estimated at 220 mg/100 g based on vegetable and chicken broth composition. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat data not available in the analogue source at this level of dish specificity.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous food: rice vermicelli noodle soup (GI ~53, low-medium boundary); adjusted downward to 52 for diluted hot pot format with protein and vegetable bulk
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lẩu Gà Lá Giang. The closest approved analogous food is rice vermicelli noodle soup (GI ~53) from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (peer-reviewed, published in AJCN). GI is estimated at 52 (low category) reflecting the diluted hot pot format, significant protein content (8 g/100 g), dietary fibre (1.33 g/100 g), and vegetable bulk, all of which attenuate glycaemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (52) × carbohydrate per serving (40 g from 400 g serving × 10 g/100 g) ÷ 100 = 20.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g; estimated 1200 mg per 400 g individual serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit broth consumption to reduce sodium intake, request reduced fish sauce in preparation, and avoid adding condiments at the table, as a full individual serving may contribute approximately 1200 mg sodium — over half the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~52), moderate carbohydrate (10 g/100 g), protein 8 g/100 g, fibre 1.33 g/100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish has a low estimated glycaemic index (~52) and moderate carbohydrate content; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should prioritise chicken, vegetables, and mushrooms over vermicelli noodles, limit noodle portion to control total carbohydrate load, and monitor overall serving size as broth-based communal dishes can encourage overconsumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.67 g/100 g (low), cholesterol 26.67 mg/100 g, sodium 300 mg/100 g (borderline)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is relatively low in saturated fat (1.67 g/100 g) and involves no deep-frying, making it a heart-friendly preparation method; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate broth intake to limit sodium exposure and request reduced fish sauce to keep the sodium contribution within cardioprotective targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory ingredients (lemongrass, garlic, mushrooms, lá giang polyphenols, vitamin C 5.33 mg/100 g); low saturated fat; no deep-frying
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The dish contains multiple anti-inflammatory ingredients including lá giang leaves (polyphenol-rich), mushrooms (beta-glucans), tomatoes, and leafy greens; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade vegetables (tomato, chilli) should reduce or omit these ingredients, and request reduced fish sauce to minimise sodium-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Hot Pot with Sour Soup Creeper (Vietnamese: Lẩu Gà Lá Giang) is a traditional Vietnamese communal hot pot dish featuring tender chicken pieces simmered in a tangy, aromatic broth infused with lá giang leaves (Aganonerion polymorphum, also called sour soup creeper), which impart a distinctive natural sourness without the use of tamarind or vinegar. The broth is typically seasoned with lemongrass, garlic, tomatoes, and fish sauce, and served at the table in a simmering pot alongside fresh herbs, leafy greens, mushrooms, and rice vermicelli noodles. Diners cook additional ingredients directly in the broth and assemble individual bowls to taste. The flavour profile is sour, savoury, and lightly herbaceous, with a clean broth texture and tender chicken. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 120 kcal per 100 g, with 8 g protein, 5.33 g fat (of which 1.67 g is saturated), and 10 g carbohydrate. Sodium is 300 mg/100 g, reflecting the fish sauce base. The dish has an estimated low glycaemic index (~52) due to the diluted noodle format, protein content, and vegetable bulk. It is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when noodle portions are controlled, and is a heart-friendly preparation due to the absence of deep-frying and low saturated fat content. The dish is naturally dairy-free and typically gluten-free when rice vermicelli is used. It is a popular dish in southern and central Vietnam and is increasingly available in Vietnamese restaurants across Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: No approved primary source contains a direct entry for Lẩu Gà Lá Giang. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central chicken soup with vegetables as the closest structural analogue, with the input record values (from an AI-generated GrabFood record, confidence 0.7) found to be broadly consistent with the USDA analogue. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite dish nature, the absence of a direct approved-source match, and the reliance on analogue estimation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Vietnamese hot pot dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central chicken soup analogues or FAO/INFOODS SEA tables at the required level of dish specificity. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this broth-based composite dish is not reported in the analogue source at this level of specificity. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA chicken soup analogue at the composite dish level and no approved source provides dish-level data for this specific Vietnamese preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.03 g/100 g (trace level from chicken fat); this is a trace value consistent with naturally occurring trans fats in poultry and is not from industrial hydrogenation. potassium_mg estimated at 220 mg/100 g based on vegetable and chicken broth composition from USDA SR Legacy data; this is an estimation and not a directly measured value. The input record listed 'country_of_origin' as Singapore, which is inconsistent with the dish being a traditional Vietnamese preparation; region_of_origin has been corrected to Vietnam. Ethnic tags 'Pho' and 'Banh Mi' in the original input are not applicable to this dish and have been removed. Allergen note: fish sauce contains fish allergen; rice vermicelli is typically gluten-free but brand verification is recommended. Halal status is assumed based on typical preparation with chicken and fish sauce; pork-free preparation should be confirmed with the specific vendor.
